highlights:
Senior AI/Machine Learning Engineer Job in Orlando | Lockheed Martin
[...]
Machine-Learning Alert

# Senior AI/Machine Learning Engineer
[...]
$90,000 - 130,000
[...]
Golf Channel Drive 7610, Orlando
[...]
- We require a bachelors degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or a closely related discipline, plus at least 5 years of professional experience; alternatively, we accept a masters degree with 3 years of experience. - We look for substantial experience applying machine learning methods to business challenges, including natural language processing, deep learning, anomaly detection, and reinforcement learning. - We require hands-on programming experience with Python, TypeScript, or compiled languages such as C++ and Golang. - We expect experience in software development and the use of sound software engineering practices. - We require strong analytical and critical-thinking abilities. - We value proven problem-solving skills and a business-oriented mindset. - We need excellent communication skills to understand business needs and explain the value of AI-driven solutions. - We require the ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams and help move projects to completion. - We require candidates to be U.S. citizens.
[...]
## Responsibilities
[...]
- We partner with business stakeholders to uncover opportunities and design AI-powered solutions. - We build statistical and machine learning models to address complex business and engineering problems. - We deploy AI capabilities and manage initiatives through to successful completion. - We apply best-in-class practices from machine learning and software engineering. - We create knowledge resources that share best practices with other AI and data science teams. - We meet with data science leaders to share guidance and discuss proven methods. - We collaborate across functions to rapidly test ideas during discovery and advance them into actionable projects. - We lead the AI Consulting portfolio within our Artificial Intelligence Center.
[...]
We are Lockheed Martins Artificial Intelligence Center (LAIC), where we develop and deploy AI solutions that create business value and support innovation across the enterprise. This leadership role offers the opportunity to shape our AI Consulting portfolio while working at the intersection of software engineering, machine learning, and business strategy. We offer flexible work schedules, including onsite and remote options, along with comprehensive benefits that support your future and security. Our culture encourages employees to think boldly, lead with a growth mindset, and help make the impossible possible as part of a collaborative, high-impact team.

results.title: AI/ML Engineer Job in Fort Worth | Lockheed Martin
highlights:
AI/ML Engineer Job in Fort Worth | Lockheed Martin
[...]
Machine-Learning Alert
[...]
# AI/ML Engineer
[...]
$90,000 - 130,000
[...]
Throckmorton Street 1000, Fort Worth
[...]
- We require a bachelors degree in a relevant discipline such as computer science, electrical engineering, mathematics, or a related engineering field. - We are looking for at least 5 years of experience in artificial intelligence or a closely related area. - We need a strong foundation in software engineering, AI, and machine learning. - We value solid expertise in signal processing theory and practical applications, including radar, communications, electronic warfare, sonar, audio, or biomedical domains. - We expect experience with modern AI methods such as computer vision, deep learning, and reinforcement learning. - We prefer proficiency in programming languages such as Python, Java, or C++. - We prefer hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX, or Keras. - We prefer experience using Python libraries such as NumPy, OpenCV, scikit-learn, pandas, and SciPy. - We look for self-motivation, a strong work ethic, and effective time management. - We value excellent interpersonal and communication skills.
[...]
## Responsibilities
[...]
- We develop and apply AI technologies to address complex signal processing challenges and deliver innovative solutions. - We contribute across the full system development lifecycle, including requirements definition, system and software design, implementation, integration, and flight testing. - We take ownership of critical assignments and lead technical teams to produce high-quality outcomes. - We help craft proposals and white papers to support funding for AI research and development efforts. - We serve in technical lead roles and mentor junior engineers to strengthen their capabilities. - We collaborate with cross-functional partners to build and deploy AI solutions. - We explain complex technical ideas and solution concepts to non-technical stakeholders. - We work with other teams across our organization to identify and advance opportunities for AI adoption.
[...]
We are part of Lockheed Martins Artificial Intelligence Center and our Applied AI team focuses on developing technologies and products that use artificial intelligence to deliver advanced signal processing capabilities to our customers. We offer the opportunity to work on high-impact programs ranging from early-stage research to deployment in a fast-paced, innovative, and collaborative environment. We also support professional growth through mentorship and development opportunities. Preferred work locations are our facilities in Fort Worth, Texas, or Palmdale, California, and remote arrangements may be considered for qualified candidates.

- Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or related field
- Proficient in programming languages such as Python or R
- Experience with machine learning algorithms and deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow and PyTorch

- Experience in LLMs and GenAI
- Ability to design, develop, and deploy AI and machine learning solutions.
- Proficiency in data processing and analysis using tools such as SQL, R, and/or Pandas.
- US Citizenship

- Experience transitioning modern AI solutions from prototype to production, particularly GenAI solutions

- Knowledge of SAP ERP Systems, Applications and Processes and data constructs

 Lockheed Martin is a leader in aeronautics and advanced technology solutions, and they are seeking a Helix AI/ML Developer to join their team. This role involves developing, integrating, and deploying AI and machine learning algorithms for mission systems, contributing to the entire technology development life cycle from requirements generation to testing and implementation. Responsibilities Responsible for developing, integrating, and deploying autonomy and artificial intelligence algorithms for mission systems, supporting the technology development life cycle from requirements generation through development, integration, and testing, as well as research in some organizations Develops, integrates, and implements algorithms to enable perception, motion/mission planning, controls, etc. functionality in LM products and platforms Translates requirements and applies requirements to development code, integrating autonomy, AI or machine learning algorithms to LM products and platforms; Determines software methods to best acquire and execute knowledge Implements algorithms into software to train systems to recognize patterns and perform specific functions Responsible for various phases of developing and maintaining autonomy software from requirements generation, software design and development to integration, testing, troubleshooting and debugging, and implementation; Review test outcomes, conducts troubleshooting, and works to debug issues Develops human-machine interface scenarios, breaking missions into tasks; Documents interface requirements and implements human-machine interfaces Skills Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Electrical/Electronic Engineering, Applied Mathematics, or a related field 1+ years of professional software development experience with a focus on AI/ML or autonomous systems Proficiency in Python and C/C++ and hands‑on experience with at least one ML framework such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit‑learn Demonstrated ability to develop, integrate, and deploy ML models in a production environment Strong problem solving skills and ability to debug complex software issues Master's degree or higher in a relevant discipline Experience with perception (computer vision, sensor fusion) or planning and control algorithms Familiarity with real time or embedded systems, ROS, DDS, or similar middleware Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines, unit and integration testing, and version control best practices Prior exposure to supply chain, logistics, or optimization problems Experience designing human machine interfaces such as dashboards, voice or gesture control Benefits Medical Dental 401k Paid time off Work/life balance Career development Mentorship opportunities Rewards & Recognition Vision Life Insurance Short-Term Disability Long-Term Disability Flexible Spending Accounts EAP Education Assistance Parental Leave Holidays Company Overview Lockheed Martin is a global security and aerospace company that specializes in advanced technology systems, products, and services. It is a sub-organization of Lockheed Martin. It was founded in 1912, and is headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is https://www.lockheedmartin.com.

results.title: Building the AI Factory: Lessons in Scaling AI from Lockheed Martin
highlights:
Building the AI Factory: Lessons in Scaling AI from Lockheed Martin
[...]
# Lessons Learned Building an AI Factory from Lockheed Martin
[...]
In a recent presentation at the AI-Ready Data Summit, Thomas Vander Wal shared how Lockheed Martin built what they call an AI Factory. Not a single tool or model, but a full platform designed to consistently transform ideas into production-grade AI systems.
[...]
Lockheed Martin’s AI Factory is made up of three platforms:
[...]
A low-code generative AI platform for building tooling, defining agents as config, and exposing them via API-first architectures.
[...]
A code-heavy MLOps platform for machine learning engineers to manage the model lifecycle and iterate on training code.
[...]
Lockheed Martin’s AI Factory initiative began in late 2021 with a primary focus on MLOps. The effort started with a small team of five engineers, initially tackling foundational challenges such as running Kubernetes and getting Kubeflow operational.
[...]
As development progressed, it became clear that these abstractions were too low-level for machine learning engineers. In response, the team introduced Panel – an abstraction layer designed to simplify Kubernetes complexity for users. From that point, the platform began to scale rapidly.
[...]
The landscape shifted significantly in 2022 with the release of ChatGPT, making generative AI far more accessible. This triggered a strategic pivot toward enabling chat-based interfaces. The first foundational model was deployed in 2023, based on Llama 2.
[...]
By early 2024, the team extended its earlier approach by evolving a basic model API into a more feature-rich platform, resulting in the creation of Genesis.
[...]
Today, across the three core repositories, the platform has seen over 2,500 code releases. Genesis alone processes approximately eight billion tokens per day on the US instance. The organization has deployed 42 on-premises models and leverages cloud-based models based on data sensitivity.
[...]
Navigator, the centralized chat platform, surpassed 45,000 unique Lockheed Martin employees in a single month, reflecting strong internal adoption. In parallel, deployments have expanded into highly sensitive environments where central infrastructure cannot be used. The team has grown to roughly 30 engineers, marking a significant scale
[...]
its initial size.
[...]
contender had merits, lake
[...]
Internal usage of the platform played a crucial role in driving improvement. By relying on the same tools, the team developed strong user empathy and identified gaps more effectively. While MLOps workflows focused on data pipelines, catalogs, and reusable components, generative AI capabilities became embedded in daily workflows, further increasing adoption and feedback loops.
[...]
Another difficult area was abstraction design. While engineers often assume users want maximum control, many enterprise users – especially in generative AI contexts – prefer simplicity. Concepts like vector stores, while powerful, are not intuitive for non-technical users. The platform needed to abstract these complexities while maintaining flexibility.
[...]
### Data-centric AI
[...]
Looking forward, one of the most important lessons is the centrality of data. Data-centric AI remains foundational, particularly for generative AI use cases such as retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), enterprise knowledge indexing, and agent-driven workflows.
[...]
Lockheed Martin’s journey in building an AI Factory reflects a transition from “experimentation” to “industrialization.” As Thomas Vander Wal shared, the success of such a massive undertaking isn’t just about the models; it’s about the infrastructure and culture that support them.
[...]
As the landscape shifts toward data-centric AI and increasingly autonomous agents, these foundational pillars – stability, usability, and internal transparency – will remain the true north for any production-grade AI system.

results.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery) - Xtalks
highlights:
## Machine Learning Engineer, Foundation Models (Prescient / AI for Drug Discovery)
[...]
A healthier future. It's what drives us to innovate. To continuously advance science and ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need today and for generations to come. Creating a world where we all have more time with the people we love. That's what makes us Roche.Advances in AI, data, and computational sciences are transforming drug discovery and development. Roche's Research and Early Development organisations at Genentech (gRED) and Pharma (pRED) have demonstrated how these technologies accelerate R&D, leveraging data and novel computational models to drive impact. Seamless data sharing and access to models across gRED and pRED are essential to maximising these opportunities. The new Computational Sciences Center of Excellence (CoE) is a strategic, unified group whose goal is to harness this transformative power of data and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to assist our scientists in both pRED and gRED to deliver more innovative and transformative medicines for patients worldwide.The OpportunityAt Roche's AI for Drug Discovery (AIDD) group (Prescient Design), we are revolutionizing drug discovery with cutting-edge machine learning techniques. We are seeking talented engineers with a passion for building large-scale, distributed machine learning algorithms and systems that will transform the drug discovery process. AIDD's Foundation Model team is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Engineer to develop the backend of our internal agents platform and enable the next generation of AI/ML-powered drug discovery applications as part of our broader Lab-in-the-Loop approach. We are looking for someone who is not only passionate about technical problem-solving but also has a proven track record of delivering innovative solutions in AI/ML. The ideal candidate should have extensive experience in leading and managing a team of engineers and scientists, and be able to foster a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence across the broader Roche organization. The group provides a dynamic and challenging environment for multidisciplinary research, including access to heterogeneous data sources, close links to top academic institutions around the world, as well as collaborations with internal Genentech and Roche teams. In this role, you will:
[...]
- Contact
- Develop various, cutting-edge internal AI/ML-based applications for drug discovery and development.
[...]
- Develop across the lifecycle of LLMs including data engineering, pre-training, fine-tuning, post-training, and evaluation
[...]
- Deploy models in production environments, working closely with other engineers to ensure scalability and reliability
[...]
- Solve core backend challenges in ML and engineering including the design, implementation, and scaling of our data, training, and deployment pipeline
[...]
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams across both AIDD and Roche to solve complex problems in
[...]
- You have a BS/MS in Computer Science, Statistics, related field, or equivalent experience and 2+ years of industry experience in machine learning; or a PhD in Computer Science and related field and 0+ years of industry experience.
[...]
- You have demonstrated success in technical capabilities deploying machine learning models in industry-grade production environments
[...]
- Experience in back-end development for LLM-powered agents and tools, or working with Model Context Protocol (MCP).
[...]
- You have experience in developing LLM/GenAI products and are familiar with open-source AI frameworks like Open WebUI.
[...]
- Strong interest in solving problems in drug discovery and biomedical science.
[...]
- You have strong programming skills in Python and using deep learning frameworks like PyTorch
[...]
- You have extensive experience with deep learning & LLMs.
[...]
- An extensive track record of delivering innovative solutions in machine learning

results.title: Data Scientist at Booz Allen Hamilton | Jeremy
highlights:
Data Scientist at Booz Allen Hamilton | Jeremy
[...]
Data Scientist at Booz Allen Hamilton | Jeremy
[...]
Booz Allen Hamilton
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
2 weeks agoBeavercreek, OhioArlington, District of ColumbiaLexington Park, MarylandEntry LevelMid-LevelData ScienceClearance Required
[...]
Data scientist role at a defense contractor focused on turning complex structured and unstructured data into actionable intelligence across government and private sector clients; requires Secret clearance.
[...]
As a data scientist, you’re excited at the prospect of unlocking the secrets held by a data set, and you’re fascinated by the possibilities presented by IoT, machine learning, and artificial intelligence (AI). In an increasingly connected world, massive amounts of structured and unstructured data open new opportunities. As a Data Scientist at Booz Allen, you can help turn these complex data sets into useful information to solve global challenges. Across private and public sectors from fraud detection to cancer research, to national intelligence, we need you to help find the answers in the data. 
On our team, you’ll use your analytical skills and data science knowledge to create real-world impact. You’ll work closely with your clients to understand their questions and needs, and then dig into their data-rich environments to find the pieces of their information puzzle. You’ll develop algorithms and systems and use the right combination of tools and frameworks to turn sets of disparate data points into objective answers to help clients make informed decisions. Ultimately, you’ll provide a deep understanding of the data, what it all means, and how it can be used.
[...]
**You Have:**
* 2+ years of experience with data cleansing, analysis, visualization, API connections, or data mining
* 2+ years of experience analyzing structured and unstructured data
* 2+ years of experience working with Python, including leveraging several different libraries such as Pandas or Numpy
* Experience developing predictive data models, quantitative analyses, or natural language processing
* Experience using cloud data tools within AWS, Azure, or Oracle
* Experience with visualization packages, including Plotly, Seaborn, or ggplot2
* Secret clearance
* Bachelor’s degree
[...]
**Nice If You Have:**
[...]
* Experience with engineering AI solutions, including accessing Large Language Model (LLM) APIs, self-hosting LLMs, fine-tuning LLMs, or implementing Model Context Protocol
* Experience working with the War Data Platform, USAF VAULT, or Palantir Foundry
* Experience with distributed data and computing tools, including MapReduce, Hadoop, Hive, EMR, Kafka, Spark, Gurobi, or MySQL
* Experience writing Infrastructure-as-Code leveraging Terraform or similar tools
* Experience working with DoW clients
* Experience developing visually compelling PowerPoint decks and other products to support client delivery
[...]
* Possession of excellent verbal and written communication skills
[...]
* TS/SCI clearance 
**Clearance:**
[...]
security investigation and may need to meet eligibility requirements for access to classified
[...]
As part of the hiring process, we will ask you to complete an identity verification process that leverages advanced biometrics and artificial intelligence to ensure authenticity and protect against identity fraud. You are expected to be on camera during interviews and assessments. We reserve the right to take your picture to verify your identity and prevent fraud.
[...]
**Candidate AI Usage Policy**
[...]
AI is a part of our daily work at Booz Allen, and we are committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI tools. However, we want to ensure a fair candidate process based on **your **own skills and knowledge. As part of this commitment, the use of artificial intelligence (AI) or other tools to assist with responses during interviews (whether in-person or virtual) is prohibited **unless permission is explicitly provided**.

highlights:
Embedded Flight Software Engineer Sr. Staff at Lockheed Martin | Jeremy
[...]
Embedded Flight Software Engineer Sr. Staff at Lockheed Martin | Jeremy

[...]
# Embedded Flight Software Engineer Sr. Staff
[...]
1 weeks agoLittleton, ColoradoPleasanton, CaliforniaSunnyvale, CaliforniaPleasanton, TexasonsiteSeniorStaffEmbeddedClearance Required
[...]
$134k – $267k
[...]
Senior Staff embedded flight software engineer developing real-time payload control systems for next-generation spacecraft at Lockheed Martin Space, requiring active TS/SCI clearance and hands-on leadership in model-based design and hardware-software integration.
[...]
## Full Job Description
[...]
Remote Sensing is seeking an experienced Software Engineer to guide a team through developing complex Payload Flight Software (PLSW) at Lockheed Martin Space. The selected candidate will work as an embedded, real-time PLSW development and integration engineer. The next-generation payload flight software development and deployment will use modern architectural concepts, model-based methodologies and software toolchains to deploy to mission-critical code to existing single-core processors.
[...]
This person will be responsible for developing software for real time control and interaction with other hardware and software subsystems and testing in a software-only and hardware/software environments, including execution of integration tests to validate hardware to software interfaces, software to software interfaces, and functionality. The job responsibilities include analyzing the hardware, software, and system requirements and implementation; developing and testing code utilizing Model Based Design approaches; establishing development and test tools; identifying and resolving anomalies during test on various software- and system-level platforms; identifying and implementing required software modifications and participating in technical reviews/audits of software products. The candidate will primarily provide hands-on design, development and integration efforts along with perform software engineering in all phases of the software lifecycle following the program Software Development Plan (SDP).
[...]
The candidate will also participate in major program reviews and potentially provide formal program briefings to government customers. They will participate in interface working groups with the hardware development team as well as host to payload that may include the customer.
[...]
• Ability to design software solutions to address complex problems.
[...]
• Extensive experience of embedded software development using C/C++, Object Oriented Design and Real-Time programming constructs.
[...]
• Working knowledge and experience of embedded and closed loop SW systems, and the verification thereof in both software simulation/emulation and hardware-in-the loop systems.
[...]
• Tools Experience with IBM DOORS, CAMEO (SysML/UML), Wind River WorkBench, and Wind River VxWorks 6.x/7.x.
[...]
• Active TS Security Clearance.
[...]
### Preferred Skills
[...]
• Comfortable working in a fast-paced, team oriented, rapidly changing environment.
[...]
• Familiarity with spacecraft subsystems, hardware, telemetry, command & control.
[...]
• Experience integrating complex software systems with high performance and/or timing constraints.
[...]
• Demonstrated experience using the Agile methodology in the development, integration and test of complex software systems and environments.
[...]

results.title: Aerospace Engineer Job in Alabama - AL,  Professional, Scientific, and Technical Services Career,  Jobs in Leidos
highlights:
Description The Defense Sector at Leidos is seeking a talented Engineer for a full-time position in the Leidos Autonomy and Artificial Intelligence branch. You will work to solve complex engineering problems with a highly technical, multidisciplinary team consisting of aerospace engineers, computer scientists, research scientists, and statisticians. This group provides cutting-edge solutions to aerospace and defense problems such as autonomous battle management, robotic vehicle guidance, and model-based system identification. For this position you must be able to be on-site daily at our work location in Huntsville, AL. If you are not already local you would need to relocate to be considered. Primary Responsibilities: Employees in this role will work to develop solutions to aerospace and defense problems by applying a variety of engineering, AI/ML, and software technical skills. In general, employees are regularly tasked to solve open-ended research problems across several different aerospace domains. Each employee will utilize their technical skills as they work through tasks involving dynamical modeling and simulation, machine learning (e.g., supervised learning, reinforcement learning), algorithm development, and data visualization. The aforementioned skills and solutions are expected to be implemented in software via programming languages such as Python. To succeed in this role, engineers are required to leverage internal resources (e.g., software toolkits), collaborate with peers and experienced professionals, and learn independently. Each engineer should be able to comfortably and effectively communicate technical results of their analysis to peers. Basic Qualifications:
[...]
- Computer Science / Math: Understanding of machine learning basics, supervised learning, reinforcement learning, and statistics.
- Ability to perform independent research to learn new skills.

results.title: Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI) | Jobspawn
highlights:
Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI) | Jobspawn

Jobspawn.← All Jobs

# Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Posted Jun 8, 2026

Lead Data Scientist – Generative AI (GenAI)

Experience: 12–15+ Years (Data Science / AI Experience)

Employment Type: Full-Time (W2 Only)

### Location: USA (Hybrid / Onsite)

Work Authorization: H4 EAD, L2S, GC EAD, Green Card, US Citizen

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly accomplished Lead Data Scientist with strong Generative AI expertise to design and drive enterprise-scale AI solutions. The ideal candidate will have deep experience in machine learning, statistical modeling, Large Language Models (LLMs), and advanced analytics, along with strong leadership capabilities to guide teams and deliver impactful AI-driven products.

This role requires ownership of GenAI solution design, model development, experimentation, deployment, and performance optimization across enterprise environments.

- Key Responsibilities
- Define and lead GenAI and data science strategy across business use cases.
- Design and implement LLM-based applications including RAG architectures.
- Lead development of machine learning models and advanced analytics solutions.
- Perform deep exploratory data analysis (EDA) and derive actionable insights.
- Architect end-to-end ML pipelines (data ingestion → training → deployment → monitoring).
- Optimize models for accuracy, scalability, and cost efficiency.
- Collaborate with Data Engineers, ML Engineers, and Product stakeholders.
- Establish best practices for MLOps, model governance, and evaluation frameworks.
- Mentor junior data scientists and lead technical decision-making.
- Drive innovation in AI/ML and Generative AI solutions.

Required Technical Skills

- Data Science & ML
- Strong expertise in Machine Learning & Statistical Modeling
- Python (NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn)
- Feature engineering, model evaluation, and tuning

- Generative AI
- LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic, LLaMA, etc.)
- Prompt engineering and LLM orchestration
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
- LangChain / LlamaIndex / Hugging Face

- Data & Processing
- SQL
- Large-scale data handling and transformation

- Cloud & MLOps
- AWS / Azure / GCP
- MLflow / SageMaker / Azure ML
- CI/CD for ML pipelines
- Model deployment and monitoring

- Preferred Qualifications
- Experience with Deep Learning (TensorFlow / PyTorch)
- Exposure to vector databases (Pinecone, FAISS, Chroma)
- Experience with Databricks or big data platforms
- Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills

- Eligibility & Compliance
- W2 Full-Time Only
- No C2C
- No consultancy or vendor profiles

How to Apply

Share your updated resume to:

manish.g@precisiontechcorp.com

Apply for This Job

The average Lockheed Martin Data Scientist in Hagerstown, MD earns $72,500 annually. This total compensation is $57,005 less than the US average for a Data Scientist. Data Scientist salaries at Lockheed Martin in Hagerstown, MD can range from $67,000 - $78,000 with equity ranging from 25-50k.

In Hagerstown, MD, The Engineering Department at Lockheed Martin earns $7,764 more on average than the Operations Department. Comparably data has a total of 2 salary records from Lockheed Martin Data Scientists.
